baseline_run: run2 (manual kickoff after domain migration to funwithscience.net)

url_history.current_behavior: Issues HTTP 301 to https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/ (verified via curl -I on 2026-04-21 — 301 with explicit redirect_url). GSC for funwithscience-org.github.io should remain verified indefinitely so it can collect redirect signals while Google consolidates authority to the new property.

apex_landing.role: umbrella index; one card linking to dome review
apex_landing.llms_txt: present — https://funwithscience.net/llms.txt (fetched 2026-04-21): 'Fun With Science' header, single project entry linking to dome review, includes nested pointers to sub-site llms.txt and sitemap.xml
apex_landing.sitemap_xml: present — 2 URLs (apex + dome review), both lastmod 2026-04-21, priorities 0.5 and 1.0
apex_landing.robots_txt: present — Allow all + both sitemap: lines (apex sitemap + dome-review sitemap)

migration_notes.migration_type: custom domain adoption via org-level CNAME inheritance (funwithscience-org/funwithscience-org.github.io holds the CNAME); dome-model-review repo inherits and serves at funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/
migration_notes.future_projects_note: Any future funwithscience-org/<repo> with GitHub Pages enabled will auto-serve at funwithscience.net/<repo>/ with no per-repo config.

our_site.llms_txt: present at https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/llms.txt — 59 lines. Deployed by decider 2026-04-19 and survived the migration byte-identical (the file is URL-invariant; no internal URL strings needed updating). Byte-identical to monitor/social/drafts/llms.txt. 'review covers 69 dome claims (V51.1) plus additional sub-entries' remains accurate: wins.json entries.length=71 but baseWins (three-digit IDs, what counts.total tracks) =69 (two sub-entries 057b and 058b for dome numbering collisions are the 'additional sub-entries').
our_site.sitemap_xml: present — 2 URLs. Both loc entries now use https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/ (verified live: 2026-04-21 fetch). No stale github.io URL.
our_site.robots_txt: present — Allow all + sitemap: https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/sitemap.xml (verified live 2026-04-21). Canonical URL correct.
our_site.meta_description: present — 'Independent scientific review of every prediction claimed by the Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model (ECM dome model). 69 claims evaluated — 0 survive scrutiny.' counts.total=69 matches.
our_site.og_tags: present — og:url, og:title, og:description all present. og:url='https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/' (verified in docs/index.html — migration applied). og:description reads '69 predictions claimed. 0 survive independent scrutiny. 10 acknowledged failures the model doesn't count.' — matches uncounted-failures.json entries.length=10.
our_site.twitter_card: present — summary card with title and description. No migration change required (no URL string).
our_site.keywords_meta: present — same keywords list. ISS-SOCIAL-META-KEYWORDS-CLAIMCLASSES (filed 2026-04-18) still pending decider triage; proposed claim-class additions (prospective_confirmed, retrospective_structural, supportive_nonunique, strawman control) remain unincorporated.
our_site.canonical_link: present and updated — <link rel="canonical" href="https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/"> (verified in docs/index.html head).
our_site.claim_review_markup: present — schema.org ClaimReview JSON-LD. Migration-updated fields: 'url' = 'https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/'; author.url = 'https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/'. itemReviewed.url = 'https://john09289.github.io/predictions' (correct — that is the subject of review, NOT our canonical). claimReviewed = 'The Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model has 69 confirmed predictions with 94.5% accuracy'. reviewRating 1/5 ('False'). Correct against dome's current claim. Recommend operator validate at validator.schema.org per SOCIAL-NOTE-004 item 6.
our_site.structured_api: intentionally missing — one canonical page + llms.txt; ClaimReview JSON-LD serves structured-data consumers.
our_site.gsc_verification: DONE 2026-04-12 on funwithscience-org.github.io property (docs/google0fa12662dfa239fc.html was previously committed per SOCIAL-NOTE-003; local working tree doesn't show the file which suggests it is git-tracked in a branch not currently pulled, not a regression). NEW property needed: funwithscience.net as a GSC Domain property — flagged as operator action in SOCIAL-NOTE-004 item 1.
our_site.github_repo_metadata: description + 6 topics DONE and unchanged. HOMEPAGE field is STALE — funwithscience-org/dome-model-review homepage still set to 'https://funwithscience-org.github.io/dome-model-review/' (verified via public API 2026-04-21). Needs update to 'https://funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/' — flagged as operator action in SOCIAL-NOTE-004 item 4.
our_site.google_analytics: GA4 tag G-QVD21HWH5X present — grandfathered under revised NC8 ('no third-party runtime dependencies, with one grandfathered exception'). Self-reports the new URL automatically. No action required from social.
our_site.bing_indexnow: PENDING. Escalation trigger moves from 2026-04-23 to 2026-04-24 (Bing Webmaster TXT verification pending until tomorrow per operator note). IndexNow key file still absent.
our_site.drift_noted_today: data/sections.json part2b prose still contains the hardcoded '69/(69+4+8) ≈ 85.2%'. uncounted-failures.json = 10 entries; correct formula is 69/(69+4+10) ≈ 83.1%. Unchanged from run1 report-2026-04-21. Draft patch at monitor/social/drafts/sections-json-2b1-formula-patch.md remains outstanding. Migration does not change this item.
our_site.notes: Manual-kickoff run2: domain migration completed today. All deployed machine-readable surfaces (canonical link, og:url, ClaimReview url/author.url, sitemap, robots, apex index) verified live on funwithscience.net. Old URL returns 301 — inbound link juice transfers. Operator-hand items now on the checklist: (1) verify funwithscience.net as GSC Domain property, (2) Bing Webmaster (TXT retry tomorrow), (4) update GitHub homepage field, (6) validate ClaimReview against the new canonical URL.

dome_site.llms_txt: present — unchanged since 2026-04-17 (last-modified header is GitHub Pages serve-time, not publish-time; content-length 3257 matches baseline).
dome_site.ai_manifest: present — V51.1, last_updated 2026-04-17 (re-verified this run). counts block {confirmed:69, prospective_confirmed:10, refined:4, registry_accuracy_pct:94.5}. agent_rules block still routes AI readers through methodology.json and review-response.html before wins/predictions — AI-steering pattern we continue to reject.
dome_site.robots_txt: present — Allow all + sitemap ref, unchanged.
dome_site.structured_api: present — 12 JSON/CSV endpoints including api/current/methodology.json, data/daily_review.json (regenerated daily via Groq pipeline), data/prediction_hashes.json.
dome_site.daily_ai_review_pipeline: present — .github/workflows/daily-ai-review.yml (Groq llama-3.3-70b). Ran today via auto-pipeline commits — routine.
dome_site.claim_index_integrity_notes: present — unchanged. Documents WIN-058 and WIN-062 visible-label collisions via stable claim_key.
dome_site.repo_metadata: description=null, homepage=null, topics=[], stars=0, forks=0, watchers=0, open_issues=1. Unchanged from run1. Dome author has made no move to add repo-level SEO metadata.
dome_site.notes: STATIC since 2026-04-17 on all author-authored infrastructure. Latest Nhughes09 commit 2026-04-17T23:31:21Z (~4 days). Today's commits (10 most recent, fetched this run) are exclusively scheduled bots: 'GitHub Action Auto-update ECM status' dominating at ~6/10, 'ECM Auto-Pipeline' 2/10, no 'ECM Daily Review Groq' in the last 10 (earlier today at 07:06 UTC per run1). Interpretation unchanged: 2026-04-17 burst was a one-off AI-steering investment.

closed_gaps_since_previous_baseline: Canonical URL migration: og:url, canonical link, sitemap URLs, robots sitemap line, llms.txt deploy URL, ClaimReview url and author.url all now use funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/. Verified live. Closes the 'github.io path is low-branding' soft disadvantage.
Apex umbrella index published at funwithscience.net/ with its own sitemap.xml, robots.txt, llms.txt. Closes the 'no parent surface for cross-project signals' gap.
301 redirect from old URL preserves any inbound link equity accumulated during the github.io phase.
new_gaps_opened_since_previous_baseline: GSC verification on funwithscience.net Domain property is pending operator action. The existing GSC property for funwithscience-org.github.io should be kept verified (per operator note) so it can collect redirect signals while Google consolidates authority, but the new Domain property is where future sitemap submissions and URL Inspection pushes will happen.

Bing Webmaster Tools verification on funwithscience.net is pending (operator attempted today via GoDaddy DNS TXT; retry tomorrow). Change of Address import from the github.io property should happen after verification.
funwithscience-org/dome-model-review repo homepage field is stale (still points at the github.io URL). Operator action — one-line gh API call or GitHub About panel edit.

ClaimReview JSON-LD should be revalidated at validator.schema.org against the new canonical URL — low risk since only url/author.url changed, but worth confirming before relying on Google's ClaimReview pickup.
remaining_gaps_we_will_not_close: llms-full.txt — dome has extended guide; our llms.txt is 59 lines and covers full methodology. Split risks drift. Revisit past 200 lines.
ai_manifest.json agent_rules — AI-steering pattern we explicitly reject.
12 machine-readable API endpoints — most exist to feed dome's own AI-steering. We don't replicate without a consumer.
review-response.html 'Strawman Control' page — meta-critique of critics; replicating is infinite-regress.
daily_review.json synthetic self-review — content-flood AI-steering; credibility hit to replicate.
our_persistent_advantages: Brandable, short canonical URL (funwithscience.net/dome-model-review/) — materially easier to pitch than github.io paths.
301 redirect inheritance — old URL behaves correctly, not a link-loss event.
ClaimReview structured data (dome still doesn't have this).
Standard SEO meta tags (description, OG, Twitter Card) — dome still lacks these.
GSC-verified legacy property (still useful for redirect signals).
GitHub repo metadata (description + 6 topics) — dome repo still null. Repo homepage pending quick update.
Apex umbrella (funwithscience.net/) gives us a home-page surface the dome does not have.
Keywords meta targeting dome-model-specific terms and author identity.

Deployed llms.txt surfacing the documentary-vs-structural distinction, the 10/9/1 three-way prospective_confirmed disagreement, and the OTS wrong-side error before an LLM absorbs the dome's anti-strawman framing.
our_soft_advantages: Editorial quality over content volume — single well-structured review vs dome's synthetic daily self-summaries.
llms.txt is descriptive (how the review is organized) rather than instructional (how LLMs should frame it) — cleaner signal for an AI system making a good-faith assessment.

Meta tags, OG, and ClaimReview are all template-driven and auto-update from wins.json / uncounted-failures.json at build time — the mistake pattern we criticize in the dome. The last remaining prose-level hardcode (sections.json part2b) is now flagged with a concrete draft patch.
Google Analytics grandfathered exception (GA4 G-QVD21HWH5X) gives us an objective signal for when discoverability converts to first organic arrivals — a measurement foothold we did not have before.
